data.title: Baxter rises as Q4 results exceed guidance
data.description:
Shares of Baxter International (NYSE:BAX) traded higher in the premarket on Thursday after the medical product maker reported Q4 2024 and full-year results surpassing its prior projections.

With the recent sale of its kidney care business complete, the Deerfield, Illinois-based firm reported $2.75B in revenue from continuing operations for Q4, indicating ~1% YoY growth on a reported basis compared to its prior projection of a low single-digit decline.

Baxter’s (NYSE:BAX) overseas sales drove the revenue beat, adding ~$1.24B to the top line with ~1% YoY growth, while its U.S. sales remained flat at about $1.51B.

The company’s adjusted diluted EPS from continuing operations fell ~11% YoY to $0.58, exceeding the original guidance of $0.50 to $0.53 per share thanks to, among other things, a lesser negative impact from Hurricane Helene that hit BAX’s North Cove, NC, production site in September.

For the full year, the company reported $1.89 of adj. earnings per share from cont. operations with ~11% YoY growth on $10.64B of revenue, which indicated a ~3% YoY rise.

Updating on the impact of Hurricane Helene, the company said all 10 production lines at its North Cove production facility that were shut down after the hurricane have resumed operations, and the facility is operating at pre-hurricane levels.

As for the 2025 outlook from cont. operations, Baxter (NYSE:BAX) projects 5%–6% YoY growth in revenue on a reported basis and $2.45–$2.55 of adjusted earnings per share.

Its revenue and adj. earnings guidance for Q1 cont. operations stood at 3%-4% YoY growth on a reported basis and $0.47 to $0.50 of adjusted earnings per share.

data.title: Aspire Biopharma announces public listing on Nasdaq
data.description:
• Aspire Biopharma (NASDAQ:ASBP) completed its business combination with SPAC PowerUp Acquisition on February 17, 2025, with proceeds totaling ~$3.8M.
  • Aspire's common stock and warrants will begin trading on the Nasdaq Global Market under ticker symbols "ASBP" and "ASBPW" on February 20, 2025.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Tesla is cited for workplace safety violations as oversight under the Trump Administration begins
data.description:
Federal regulators cited Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) for violating workplace safety rules at the Gigafactory in Austin, Texas. The U.S. Department of Labor confirmed the findings in a statement provided to Reuters. The department did not disclose the specific nature of Tesla's (NASDAQ:TSLA) workplace-safety failures or if any fines were imposed.

OSHA launched the investigation immediately following the August 1 death of Victor Gomez Sr., an electrician who was working as a contractor at the Tesla (TSLA) plant in Texas. Reports indicate that Gomez was killed while inspecting electrical panels at the site. A lawsuit filed against Tesla (TSLA) alleged negligence by the company because the panel was supposed to be inactive.

"The investigation is closed and citations have been issued," said the department, which oversees the agency that conducted the probe, the U.S. Occupational Safety and Health Administration.

The case against Tesla (TSLA) is one of the first to be resolved during the Trump Administration, amid questions on how the Elon Musk-led company will fare with government investigations and regulatory oversight of its autonomous driving technology.

On Seeking Alpha, Investing Group Leader Victor Dergunov is still bullish on Tesla (TSLA), but thinks the EV stock could see headwinds due to lower than expected sales for its vehicles and other products, which could impact the price negatively, especially during this "current uncertainty filled stage."

Shares of Tesla (TSLA) edged 0.3% higher in premarket trading on Thursday to $361.52 vs. the 52-week range of $138.80 to $488.54.

data.title: Short interest on S&P 500 Industrials dips in January
data.description:
Short sellers cut their bets against the S&P 500 industrial sector stocks in January, with the S&P 500 Industrial ETF (NYSEARCA:XLI) gaining almost 5% during the month.

As of January's end, the average short interest rate in S&P 500 industrials slipped to 2.04%, down 9 bps from 2.13% at the end of December.

The S&P 500's industrial sector (NYSEARCA:XLI) gained 5%, compared to the broader S&P 500, which was up 2.7% in January.

Short interest, which could be an indicator of pessimism, calculates the number of shares sold against the company float. (Short Interest % = Number of Shares Sold Short ÷ Stock Float).

Most shorted stocks:

Southwest Airlines (LUV) - 5.93% vs. 5.65% last month.

Dayforce (DAY) - 5.10% vs 7.31%.

CH Robinson Worldwide (CHRW) - 4.57% VS 4.25%.

Amentum Holdings (AMTM) - 4.55% vs 3.92%.

United Airlines Holding (UAL) - 4.50% vs 6.07%.

Least shorted stocks:

Parker-Hannifin (PH) - 0.81% vs 1.01% last month.

RTX Corporation (RTX) - 0.84% vs 0.88%.

AMETEK (AME) - 0.86% VS 1.12%.

Broadridge Financial Solutions (BR) - 0.87% vs 0.79%.

Cummins (CMI) - 0.97% vs 1.34%.

Short interest as per industries:

Within the industry, Software remained the most shorted industry within the industrial sector, with 5.12% short interest at the end of January, compared to 6.83% last month.

Passenger Airlines were the second most shorted industry with a short interest of 5.01% compared to 6.00% at the end of December.

Industrial Conglomerates is the least shorted sector with short interest of 1.15% at the end of January.

data.title: NewGenIvf Group receives approval for continued Nasdaq listing; shares rise
data.description:
• NewGenIvf Group Limited (NASDAQ:NIVF) announced that Nasdaq has granted the company's request for continued listing.
  • The company intends to apply for transition to Nasdaq Capital Market on or before February 26, 2025.
  • NIVF shares rise 22% premarket on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Talkspace misses top-line estimates; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Talkspace press release (NASDAQ:TALK): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.01 in-line.
  • Revenue of $48.72M (+14.9% Y/Y) misses by $1.22M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $2.7 million, an improvement from $(0.3) million adjusted EBITDA loss in the fourth quarter of 2023.

  • Financial Guidance

    The following guidance is based on current market conditions and expectations and the information available to the Company today. For 2025, Talkspace expects:

    • Revenue to be in the range of $220 million to $235 million vs $232.79M consensus
    • Adjusted EBITDA to be in the range of $14 million to $20 million

data.title: Walmart stock stumbles on soft guidance
data.description:
Walmart’s value proposition in an inflationary environment continued to resonate with an increasing number of consumers as the retail giant’s revenue increased by 4.2% in the fiscal fourth quarter of 2025, driving profits up 10%, both of which beat Wall Street’s expectations.

Shares are trading defensively, however, as the company’s outlook for the current quarter and FY26 failed to meet Wall Street’s lofty expectations.

“The slide in Walmart's share price following the release of its Q4 results could be attributable to its premium trading valuation and the lack of associated wiggle room. Nonetheless, Q4 results show that WMT is still able to demonstrate sales and earnings competency despite a challenged macroeconomic environment, one that now includes risks associated with tariffs,” Seeking Alpha analyst Justin Purohit said.

Comparable sales across all stores – excluding fuel stations – increased 4.6% from last year, with the number of transactions increasing 2.8%. Shoppers also spent more, with average ticket up another 1.8% after contracting in the prior year’s quarter. A 20% increase in online sales contributed ~290 basis points to comparable store sales. Shoppers were especially attracted to Walmart’s warehouse with Sam’s Club sales climbing 5.7% in the reported quarter, and up 7.1% excluding fuel sales, with Sam’s Club online sales continuing to gain in popularity, surging 24% from a year ago.

Accounting for the negative impact on sales from the stronger dollar, international sales were down slightly from a year ago but increased 5.7% to account for foreign exchange fluctuations.

Looking ahead to the current quarter and for the full year 2026, Walmart’s (NYSE:WMT) guidance “assumes a generally stable consumer and continued pressure from its mix of products and formats globally," the company cautioned. The expectation for Q1 sales to be up a 3% to 4% should result in a profit between $0.57 and $0.58 per share. Down 3 to 4 cents from a year ago and below the consensus estimate of $0.65.

For FY26, Walmart (NYSE:WMT) also expects sales to improve 3%-4%, translating to $698.1B at the midpoint with earnings between $2.50 to $2.60 per share. This is also less than Wall Street’s expectations for a profit of $2.76 per share on $705.14B in sales.

Shares are down 8% into Thursday's opening bell, weighing on shares of Target (TGT), Costco (COST), and Dollar General (DG)

data.title: Stock index futures slip as investors digest Walmart's earnings report
data.description:
Stock index futures slipped on Thursday as investors were left unimpressed with Walmart's (WMT) earnings report, a day after the S&P 500 notched another record high.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.1%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.3%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 2 basis points to 4.51%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 4.26%.

On the earnings front, Walmart (WMT) shares were down 8.5% in premarket trade as its profit, and sales outlook missed estimates amid stiff competition.

Palantir (PLTR) was also down 3.9% in premarket trade as Hegseth memo called for sharp spending cut.

The initial jobless claims will report before the opening bell today, and is expected to rise to 215K.

The February Philadelphia Fed manufacturing index will also land today and is anticipated to come in at 19.4.

The January leading index, which is being released during market hours, is expected to come in at -0.1% M/M.

Wall Street's major averages seesawed on Wednesday, but the benchmark S&P 500 eventually closed at a record high as investors digested the Federal Reserve's latest minutes and President Donald Trump's latest tariff action.

"The minutes of the January Federal Reserve meeting gave a cautious policy outlook. Nervousness about the economic consequences of Trump’s policies was clear. In particular, the potential for Trump to ignite a further wave of inflation was cited," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

"Markets put in a divergent performance over the last 24 hours, with U.S. assets continuing to reach fresh highs, even as the rest of the world struggled on the back of President Donald Trump’s tariff threats and concerns about Ukraine," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

data.title: Sabre reports mixed Q4 results; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Sabre press release (NASDAQ:SABR): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.08 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $715M (+4.1% Y/Y) misses by $1.6M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $115 million, an improvement versus Adjusted EBITDA of $96 million in the fourth quarter of 2023.
  • Free Cash Flow for the quarter was $67 million, versus Free Cash Flow of $77 million in the fourth quarter of 2023.
  • FY25: Revenue: $3.19B consensus

data.title: Potential DOD budget cuts give Palantir 'more opportunity,' not less: Wedbush
data.description:
If the Department of Defense goes through with the type of budget cuts suggested by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's leaked memo, it would give Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ:PLTR) “more opportunity,” not less, Wedbush Securities argued.

Shares of Palantir slid 3.5% in premarket trading, following yesterday's 10% decline.

“The bears, which have hated Palantir from $12 to $120 in the last 18 months, now have found their latest 'silver bullet' negative thesis around PLTR being exposed to these budget cuts, and we saw a sharp sell-off in trading accordingly once this news about [the Department of Defense] hit in the afternoon,” Wedbush analysts led by Dan Ives wrote in a note to clients.

“This is exactly the opposite [of] how we believe these DOD cuts will play out as in our view Palantir's unique software approach will enable the company to gain more IT budget dollars at the Pentagon....not less, despite these initial knee-jerk reactions from the Street,” the analysts continued. “Palantir is so well positioned for this new disciplined spending environment at the Pentagon, and this will ultimately be a positive growth catalyst as the various programs are scrutinized and as Karp & Co. get a bigger seat at the table in the Beltway.”

Hegseth's memo, obtained by the Washington Post, called for 8% annual spending cuts over the next five years. However, 17 categories were said to be exempted from the cuts, including security at the southern U.S. border, the modernization of the U.S. nuclear weapon arsenal and missile defense, one-way attack drones and other areas.

Delving deeper, Wedbush analysts said that the “stepped-up” investments in artificial intelligence coming from the Trump administration, namely Project Stargate, should benefit Palantir. It is in the “the sweet spot” to benefit from federal government AI-related spending, including getting FedRAMP approval for its entire cloud services, the analysts explained.

“Palantir remains one of our top names to own in 2025, and we believe this sell-off represents another opportunity with PLTR generating traction across both federal and commercial for its entire portfolio,” the analysts, who maintained their Outperform rating and $120 price target, added.

Separately, on Thursday, Palantir was initiated with a Buy rating and $141 price target at Loop Capital.
